paper_title: Archaeological and Seasonal Analysis of Oyster Shells

methods.description: Process for excavating and collecting oyster shell samples from the Conors Island shell midden for further analysis.

steps.description: Trial excavations led by Finbar McCormick took place in 2010. The site is a complex of small individual shell heaps, and has layers that have been assigned to the Mesolithic, Neolithic and Bronze Age periods.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct trial excavations at Conors Island shell midden

steps.description: All matrices were wet-sieved and floated to 0.25 mm to recover smaller classes of anthropogenic materials.
steps.searchable_summary: wet-sieve and float matrices to 0.25 mm

steps.description: The oyster shells sampled in this study were derived from two contexts (5 and 7), identified as individual shell pockets, within Layer 4.
steps.searchable_summary: collect oyster shells from specific contexts in Layer 4

methods.description: Preliminary AMS radiocarbon dating undertaken on charcoal samples to determine the age of the midden deposits.

steps.description: Preliminary AMS radiocarbon (14C) dating undertaken on two small roundwood charcoal samples from the base of the midden deposits in the cliff section adjacent to Trench 4.
steps.searchable_summary: perform AMS radiocarbon dating on charcoal samples

steps.description: When calibrated at 95.4% probability, these results yielded a Late Mesolithic age.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate radiocarbon dating results to determine age

methods.description: Preparation and analysis of oyster shells to determine the season of death through incremental growth-line analysis.

steps.description: The oysters were thin-sectioned using the method developed by Milner (2001) with some modifications.
The sectioned shells were placed face down in plastic moulds, cleaned, and coated with release agent.
steps.searchable_summary: embed sectioned shells in resin using plastic moulds

steps.description: The hinges were embedded in resin and left to cure in an environment of constant humidity and temperature overnight.
steps.searchable_summary: cure embedded shells in resin overnight

steps.description: The resin blocks were lightly ground using successively finer metallographic grit papers.
steps.searchable_summary: grind resin blocks with metallographic grit papers

steps.description: The surfaces of the resin blocks were polished using a Texmet polishing cloth coated in diamond paste.
steps.searchable_summary: polish resin blocks with diamond paste

steps.description: The polished facets of the resin blocks were attached to a glass slide using adhesive and exposed to ultraviolet light to harden.
steps.searchable_summary: attach polished resin blocks to glass slide with adhesive
outputs: resin blocks attached to glass slide

steps.description: The bodies of the resin blocks were cut from the slide using a precision saw, leaving a slice of the embedded hinge on the glass slide.
steps.searchable_summary: cut resin blocks to leave hinge slice on glass slide
inputs: resin blocks attached to glass slide

steps.description: The shell and resin were lightly ground to produce the thin section, monitored by eye and microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: grind shell and resin to produce thin section

steps.description: The thin sections were examined at magnifications between ×10 and ×40 to assess the season of death.
steps.searchable_summary: examine thin sections under microscope to assess season of death
